title: LEARNING OPTIMIZED METALABEL EMBEDDED RANGE SEARCH STRUCTURES
summary:
A method in a data processing system and apparatus for organizing electronic data, structured or unstructured, with associated metalabels, on a recordable medium of a data processing system. The data items are structured in a heterogeneous metalabel search structure. Searching the plurality of metalabel search structures can then be done with a heterogenous-range query including at least one term and one range query. The query can also be specified in a natural language which is converted to the specific heterogenous-range query. Also provided is a method for searching using geometric shapes that are mapped into range-string queries where the ranges are d-dimensional hyper-rectangles or cubes that cover the geometric shape. A method to aid the selection of the appropriate shape is provided as well as a method to cost-efficiently select the hyper-rectangles and cubes to cover the shape.

ABSTRACT
A method in a data processing system and apparatus for organizing electronic data, structured or unstructured, with associated metalabels, on a recordable medium of a data processing system. The data items are structured in a heterogeneous m
etalabel search structure. Searching the plurality of metalabel search structures can then be done with a heterogenous-range query including at least one term and one range query. The query can also be specified in a natural language which is converted to the specific heterogenous- range query. Also provided is a method for searching using geometric shapes that are mapped into range-string queries where the ranges are d-dimensional hyper-rectangles or cubes that cover the geometric shape. A method to aid the selection of the appropriate shape is provided as well as a method to cost-efficiently select the hyper-rectangles and cubes to cover the shape.

AMENDMENT TO THE CLAIMS
1
1. (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method comprising a data processor for organizing and storing available data using metalabelst
he method comprising:organizing the data using a plurality of heterogeneous hierarchical metalabels, associated with each data item wherein a heterogeneous hierarchical metalabel comprises an ordered sequence of a plurality of atomic-labels separated by a delimiter, each atomic-label being either an element or an n-dimensional vector of elements, the elements chosen from an ordinal set;
storing the data using a plurality of nodes in a heterogeneous-range-tree structure;
wherein the search structure for subsequent atomic labels sep
arated by delimiters is embedded in the search structure of the p
revious atomic label in the ordered seq
uence,

each data item being identified by a plurality of metalabels, with all the metalabels identifying a common data item linked together;receiving a heterogenous-range query from a user, the query comprising a plurality of atomic-labels or range atomic-labels separated by a delimiter; wherein a range atomic- label is an ordered pair chosen from an ordinal set;
searching the heterogeneous range-tree structure for user-defined metalabels matching the hierarchical search term;
wherein every atomic-label is matched with the corresponding node in the tree structure, and every range atomic-label in the metalabel is matched with at least one range structure nodes in an n-dimensional range structure wherein for each dimension, the range of the heterogenous range query matches the range of each of the corresponding dimensions at the range structure node; and
returning at least one data item from the matched nodes to the user.

Currently amended

13. (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method to compute queries for data associated with heterogenous hierarchical metalabels using a data processor for operating a neural network, the method comprising:
receiving a query from a user, wherein the query is specified in natural language;
using the neural network to analyze the heterogenous hierarchical metalabels assigned to the data to determine corresponding structure;
wherein the hierarchical metalabel is an ordered sequence of atomic labels;

using the neural network to convert the user query to a heterogenous range-tree search query;
wherein the neural network uses the metalabel rang
e structures and a
loss function to op
timize the metalabel range q
uery;

using the heterogenous range-tree search query to search for user-defined metalabels matching the hierarchical search term in the heterogeneous range-tree structure that stores the data;
wherein the search structure for subsequent atomic labels is embedded in the search structure of the p
revious atomic label in the ordered sequence, each data item being
 identified by a p
lurality of hierarchical metalabels,

and

returning at least one data item matching the user query.

15. (Currently amended) A computer-implemented method comprising a data processor for storing and searching data using metalabelst
he method comprising:
storing the metalabels that are range-strings, wherein a range-string comprises an ordered sequence of at least one string and at least one n-dimensional range, each range represented by at least a minimum and maximum value and each string and range separated from preceding string or range by a delimiter;
storing the data using a plurality of nodes in a range-string tree structure;
wherein a plurality of n-dimensional range search data structures is embedded at each of a plurality of nodes of the string search tree structure.
receiving a shape range-string query from a user,
 wherein the shape range-string search comprises at least one string term and at least one geometric structure;
Covering the received shape range by a plurality of n-dimensional ranges and using these ranges to search for metalabels satisfying the shape range-string query for user-defined metalabels; and
returning at least one data items of the matched range structure nodes to the user.

Currently amended
